The Philistines were punished for seven months because of the ark’s presence; it was a plague to them so long because they would not send it home sooner. Sinners prolong their own miseries by refusing to give up their sins. The Israelites made no effort to recover the ark. Alas! where will we find concern for religion above all other matters? In times of public calamity, we fear for ourselves, our families, and our country; but who cares for the ark of God? We are blessed with the gospel, but it is treated with neglect or contempt. We need not wonder if it should be taken from us; to many people this, even in times of great suffering, would occasion no grief. There are multitudes whom any belief would please as well as Christianity. But there are those who value God’s house, word, and ministry above their richest possessions, who dread the loss of these blessings more than death. How willing bad men are to avoid their convictions, and when they are in trouble, to believe it is a chance that happens; and that the rod has no voice which they should hear or heed!

These two cows knew their owner, their great Owner, whom Hophin and Phinehas did not know. God’s providence takes notice even of animals, and serves its own purposes by them. When the reapers saw the ark, they rejoiced; their joy for that was greater than the joy of harvest. The return of the ark, and the revival of holy ordinances, after days of restraint and trouble, are matters of great joy.

It is a great affront to God for arrogant people to pry into, and meddle with the secret things which do not belong to them. Man was ruined by desiring forbidden knowledge. God will not allow his ark to be profaned. Do not be deceived; God is not mocked. Those who will not fear his goodness, and reverently use the tokens of his grace, shall feel his justice. The number struck down is expressed in an unusual manner in the original, and it is likely that it means 1170. They desire to be rid of the ark. Foolish people rush from one extreme to another. They should rather have asked, How may we have peace with God, and recover his favor? Thus, when the word of God works with terror on sinners’ consciences, they, instead of taking the blame and shame to themselves, quarrel with the word, and reject it. Many stifle their convictions, and put salvation away from them.
